content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
December 21,
1993
Charles Adams
420 South Joaquin
Coalinga,
CA
93210
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—93-446
Dear Mr.
Adams:
You have requested advice regarding the conflict—of—interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”)
1 as they
pertain to your position as a trustee on the Coalinga—Huron
Unified School Board.
The information provided in this letter is based on our
telephone conversation on December 20,
1993.
You have reviewed
and verified the facts set forth below as being accurate.
Please
note that immunity is limited to the specific facts contained in
this letter.
(Regulation 18329(b) (7).)
QUESTION
May you participate in the decision to adopt the “Five Year
Facilities Plan” to levy school impact fees for new construction
in your school district?
CONCLUSION
You may not participate in the decision to adopt the “Five
Year Facilities Plan” to levy school impact fees if the decision
will have a material financial effect on Spano Enterprises, Inc.,
a source of income to you.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—93—446
Page 2
FACTS
You are a trustee on the Coalinga—Huron Unified School Board
(the “school board”).
You are also a licensed real estate broker
and work for Coalinga Realty.
You are an independent contractor
with Coalinga Realty and are paid on a commission basis.
You do
not have any ownership interest in this real estate company.
A developer,
Spano Enterprises, Inc.,
(“Spano Enterprises”)
was a source of commission income to you of more than $250 in
April of 1993.
You were the listing agent for Posa Chanet,
a
residential subdivision which Spano Enterprises developed.
Spano
Enterprises is currently adding another phase to the Posa Chanet
subdivision; another realty company is the listing agent for this
new phase of the subdivision at this time.
Although you are no
longer the listing agent for Posa Chanet,
you could represent a
client/buyer who is interested in purchasing a home in Posa
Chanet.
On December 21,
1993, the board will be voting to adopt a
“Five Year Facilities Plan” (“plan”), which among other things
will levy a fee on construction to finance future school
construction.
The proposed fee will be $1.65 per square foot of
any new residential construction, including new construction and
home additions,
in the Coalinga and Huron school district area.
The fee will be paid directly to the school district.
As part ‘of
the five year plan, the board is also considering fees on new
commercial and industrial construction and additional ‘mitigation
measures.
Although we do not have any information on the other
construction fees or the additional mitigation measures,
you
stated that it is possible that additional mitigation could
involve land swaps or other measures which could affect developers
differently.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making,
participating in making,
or using his or her official position to
influence a governmental decision in which the official knows or
has reason to know the official has a financial interest.
An official has a financial interest in a governmental deci
sion within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial ef
fect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally,
on
the official, or on a member of the official’s immediate family,
or on:
(c)
Any source of income,
other
than
gifts
and
other
than
loans
by
a
commercial
lending
institution in the regular course
of
business
on
terms
available
to
the
public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty dol
lars
($250)
or more in value provided to, received


File No. A-93-446
Page 3
by or promised to the
public
official
within
12
months prior to the time when the decision is made.
Section 87103(c).
You receive commission income for your services as a real
estate broker.
Regulation 18704.3 (c) (3) states that the following
are sources of commission income to a real estate broker:
(A)
The person the broker represents in the
transaction;
(B)
If the broker receives a commission from a
transaction conducted by an agent working under the
broker’s auspices, the person represented by the agent;
(C)
Any brokerage business entity through which
the broker conducts business; and
(0)
Any person who receives a finder’s or other
referral fee for referring a party to the transaction to
the broker, or who makes a referral pursuant to a
contract with the broker.
Regulation 18704.3(c) (2).
Spano Enterprises is a source of commission income to you of
more than $250 in the past twelve months.
If it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision to adopt the five year plan to levy
school impact fees will have a material financial effect on Spano
Enterprises, you may not participate in the decision to adopt the
plan unless the public generally exception applies.
Material Financial Effect
Regulation 18702.2
(copy enclosed) sets forth financial
standards which you must apply to determine if there will be a
material financial effect on a business entity indirectly involved
in a decision.
2
Although we do not know which category of the
regulation applies to Spano Enterprises, it appears that the
impact fee, the additional mitigation measures or any other aspect
of the five year plan may have a material financial effect upon
Spano Enterprises.
If so,
you may not participate in any
decisions regarding the five year plan, unless the public
generally exception applies.
2
Please note that the Commission does not act as the finder of
fact in providing advice.
(In re Ogelsby)
1 FPPC Ops.
71.)


File No.
A—93—446
Page 4
Public Generally•
Even if a governmental decision will have a material
financial effect on a public official’s economic interest, the
official may still participate in the decision if the effect on
the official’s interest is indistinguishable from the effect on
the public generally.
(Regulation 18703,
copy enclosed.)
For the “public generally” exception to apply, the decison
must affect the official’s economic interest in substantially the
same.manner as it will affect a significant segment of the public
in the jurisdiction.
Accordingly,
you may only participate in the
board’s decision to adopt the five year plan if the effect on
Spano Enterprises is substantially the same as the effect on the
public generally.
Regulation 18703 provides objective standards which define
what constitutes a “significant segment” of the public generally
with respect to a variety of situations.
The regulation also
provides an exception applicable to rates and assessments.
(Regulation 18703 (b).)
Regulation 18703(a) sets forth the general rule that the
material financial effect of a governmental decision on a public
official’s economic interests is indistinguishable from its effect
on the public generally if both subdivisions
(a) (1)
and
(a) (2)
apply.
These subdivisions define “significant segment” and
“substantially the same manner” as follows:
(1)
Significant Segment:
The governmental decision
will affect a “significant segment” of the public
generally as set forth below:
(A)
The decision will affect:
(i)
Ten percent or more of the
population in the jurisdiction of the
official’s agency or the district the official
represents, or
(ii)
Ten percent or more of all property
owners, all home owners,
or all households in
the jurisdiction of the official’s agency or
the district the official represents,
or
(iii)
Fifty percent of all businesses in
the jurisdiction or the district the official
represents,
so long as the segment is composed
of persons other than a single industry,
trade,
or profession; or,
(B)
The decision will affect 5,000


File No.
A—93—446
Page 5
individuals who are residents of the
jurisdiction; or,
*****
(2) Substantially the Same Manner:
The governmental
decision will affect the official’s economic interest in
substantially the same manner as it will affect the
economic interests of the segment identified in
subdivision
(a) (1)
of this regulation.
The special rule set forth in subdivision
(b)
of the
regulation sets forth an exception for rates and assessments
whereby “the financial effect of a governmental decision on an
official’s economic interest is indistinguishable from its
financial effect on the public generally if..”:
(1)
The decision is to establish or
adjust assessments, taxes, fees,
charges,
or
rates or other similar aecisions which are
applied on a proportional basis on a
significant segment of the jurisdiction as
defined in subdivision
(a) (1)
of this
regulation.
The special rule may only be applied to rates which are
-
applied on a proportional basis to a significant segment of the
jurisdiction as defined in subdivision
(a) (1).
To the extent that
the “Five Year Facilities Plan” only levies a fee for new
residential construction which is applied on a proportional basis
to a significant segment of the jurisdiction, the public generally
exception may apply.
However,
although we do not have any information on the
specific details of the “Five Year Facilities Plan,” you have
indicated that there are additional mitigation measures which may
apply to developers.
Therefore, since the entire plan encompasses
more than just an impact fee, the exception for rate decisions
does not apply.
In conclusion,
since the “Five Year Facilities Plan” is being
adopted as a total package plan, it is not possible for us to
apply the “public generally” exception given the limited facts and
information we have received.
However, with the limited
information we do have, it does not appear that the public
generally exception applies since the plan is much broader than
just a uniform fee on residential construction.
Therefore, if the
decision will have a material financial effect on Spano
Enterprises, you may not participate in the school board decision
to adopt the “Five Year Facilities Plan.”


File No.
A—93—446
Page
6
I trust this answers your question.
SGC:JS:ak
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Legal Division
Enclosures

in your school district?

File No.

QUESTION: May you participate in the decision to adopt the “Five Year
Facilities Plan” to levy school impact fees for new construction
in your school district?

CONCLUSION: You may not participate in the decision to adopt the “Five
Year Facilities Plan” to levy school impact fees if the decision
will have a material financial effect on Spano Enterprises, Inc.,
a source of income to you.

18000-18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

File No.
A—93—446
